rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, February 5, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. DEFENDANT GOULDS PUMPS, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is taken off calendar per request of moving party. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD PALMER VS. AC AND S INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Friction Materials Corp. Of L.A.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, February 5, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Friction Materials Co. of Los Angeles' motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is moot as to the causes of action for enterprise liability and false representation and denied as to the remaining causes of action. The hearing was continued from January 26 to give plaintiffs an opportunity to present evidence, if they were able, that the brake products supplied by Friction Materials Co. of Los Angeles to Lads from 1962-1968 contained asbestos. The declaration of William Ewing creates a triable issue of fact on this question. Mr. Ewing has sufficient foundation upon which to base his opinion that "virtually all" brake linings in the 1960's contained asbestos. Defendant failed to mention any facts in its separate statement that satisfies its burden of production on the cause of action for punitive damages. =(220/HEK)
